items.title: Kenya relocates endangered black rhinos in conservation bid | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>In a move to support the long-term survival of black rhinos, Kenya Wildlife Service has started the process of relocating 21 of the critically-endangered species to a new sanctuary.

#kenya #rhino #wildlifeconservation #africa #africanews #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Early arrival of India's monsoon rains brings heatwave relief | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Monsoon rains hit the coast of India's southernmost state of Kerala eight days earlier than usual, marking the earliest arrival in 16 years and providing the promise of a bumper harvest and relief from a gruelling heatwave.

items.title: El Chapo's former lawyer among those on the ballot to be judges in Mexico | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>When Mexicans vote in the country's first judicial elections on June 1, Silvia Delgado will be on the ballot, hoping to become a criminal-court judge. She represented the notorious drug kingpin Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán in 2016.

items.title: Diary of a Gazan family's descent toward starvation | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Mervat Hijazi and her nine children didn't eat at all on Thursday - apart from her underweight baby who had a sachet of peanut paste. Their plight is a snapshot of the misery plaguing Gaza.

items.title: 3D-printed tower unveiled in Switzerland
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Tor Alva, believed to be one of the world's tallest 3D-printed structures, was unveiled in the small Swiss village of Mulegns on May 21. Meaning "White Tower" in the Swiss regional language of Romansh, the nearly 30-meter-high, white concrete tower showcases advanced digital construction techniques.
#tower #technology #switzerland #swiss #toralva #3dprinting</article>

items.title: Judges on the ballot in Mexico
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Mexicans will vote for judges for the first time in a June 1 election. The shift from appointed judges has faced criticism, fueled by fears that organized crime may gain more influence. The government says electing judges will help root out corruption. On this episode of Reuters World News, Cassandra Garrison joins us from Mexico City to meet the judges on the ballot.


 


Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices (https://megaphone.fm/adchoices)</article>
